Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and low total CO₂, which may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Liver enzymes (ALP, ALT) are mildly elevated, possibly reflecting hepatic or biliary involvement. Triglycerides are high and HDL is low, indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Other parameters are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation and further evaluation of renal and hepatic function.
